_meta.description: 30 real AWS service tools with boto3-derived metadata. Each tool follows the Toolshed enhanced-representation pattern from Ch5/Ch6 of Agentic Graph RAG: name, description, boto3_method, parameters, synthetic_queries (LLM-generated example user intents), key_topics.
_meta.source: Agentic Graph RAG Ch5/Ch6 — Toolshed five-component representation (Zhu et al. via Anthropic agent-skills + Microsoft Core AI Platform research)

tools.name: cloudwatch_logs_insights_query
tools.description: Run a CloudWatch Logs Insights query across one or more log groups to find errors, latency spikes, or specific patterns in service logs.
synthetic_queries: show me 5xx errors in the last hour
investigate latency in the checkout API
what errors happened during the spike at 3pm

tools.name: cloudwatch_get_metric_data
tools.description: Retrieve time-series metric data from CloudWatch (CPU, memory, latency, throughput, request counts) for ECS tasks, Lambda functions, RDS instances, or any AWS-emitted metric.
synthetic_queries: what does the latency trend look like
show me request count over the last 6 hours
compare current load to last week
did memory pressure cause the slowdown

tools.name: xray_get_trace_summaries
tools.description: Retrieve X-Ray distributed trace summaries to identify slow segments, error propagation paths, and service-to-service latency in a microservices architecture.
synthetic_queries: trace the request that timed out
where in the call chain did the error happen

tools.name: lambda_get_function
tools.description: Retrieve Lambda function configuration including last-modified time, code size, memory, timeout, and environment variables. Useful for correlating deployment events with incident timelines.
synthetic_queries: when was this function last deployed
did a deploy correlate with the spike

tools.name: lambda_invoke
tools.description: Synchronously or asynchronously invoke a Lambda function with a payload. Used to trigger remediation logic, run health checks, or execute on-demand operations.

tools.name: s3_list_objects_v2
tools.description: List objects in an S3 bucket, optionally filtered by prefix. Used to inspect log archives, build artifact storage, or data-lake contents.
synthetic_queries: what files are in this bucket
show me build artifacts from today

tools.name: s3_get_object
tools.description: Download a single object from S3. Used to retrieve specific log files, build artifacts, or configuration data.

tools.name: dynamodb_query
tools.description: Query a DynamoDB table by partition key (and optional sort-key condition). Used to look up specific items by indexed attributes — orders, sessions, audit records.
synthetic_queries: look up this customer's recent orders
find session records for the user

tools.name: dynamodb_scan
tools.description: Scan a DynamoDB table reading every item (or filtered subset). Expensive on large tables — use Query when possible.
synthetic_queries: count all items in the table
find all records matching a condition

tools.name: ecs_describe_tasks
tools.description: Get detailed status of running ECS tasks: which container instance, last status transition, exit codes, stop reason. Critical for diagnosing why a service is unhealthy.
synthetic_queries: what is the status of the deployment

tools.name: ecs_list_services
tools.description: List ECS services in a cluster. Used to enumerate which workloads are deployed, find a service by name pattern, or check desired-vs-running counts.
synthetic_queries: show me everything in the cluster

tools.name: ecs_update_service
tools.description: Update an ECS service — change task count, force a new deployment, or roll back to a previous task definition.

tools.name: apigw_get_stages
tools.description: Retrieve API Gateway stage configurations including throttling settings, caching, logging level, and stage variables. Useful for correlating config changes with traffic anomalies.
synthetic_queries: what are the API Gateway throttling limits

tools.name: rds_describe_db_instances
tools.description: Describe RDS database instances: engine version, instance class, storage, status, endpoint. Used to verify database state during incidents and check for maintenance events.
synthetic_queries: what version is the database running

tools.name: rds_describe_db_log_files
tools.description: List RDS database log files available for download. Useful for inspecting slow-query logs, error logs, and general logs during database performance investigations.
synthetic_queries: show me the slow query log

tools.name: sqs_get_queue_attributes
tools.description: Retrieve SQS queue metadata: message count, in-flight count, age of oldest message, visibility timeout. Used to detect queue backlogs and consumer-lag incidents.

tools.name: sns_publish
tools.description: Publish a message to an SNS topic. Used to fan out notifications, alert on-call engineers, or trigger downstream Lambda subscribers.
synthetic_queries: send an alert to the team

tools.name: eventbridge_list_rules
tools.description: List EventBridge rules in an event bus. Used to audit scheduled tasks, find rules that fire on specific events, or check rule state (enabled/disabled).
synthetic_queries: find eventbridge rules for our service

tools.name: step_functions_describe_execution
tools.description: Describe a Step Functions state machine execution — current state, input/output, error if any. Used to debug failed workflows and trace orchestration paths.
synthetic_queries: show me the state machine input

tools.name: iam_get_role
tools.description: Retrieve IAM role details including trust policy and attached managed policies. Used during permission-related incidents to verify the role identity an agent or service is acting under.
synthetic_queries: what permissions does this role have

tools.name: iam_simulate_principal_policy
tools.description: Simulate IAM policy evaluation against a specific action to determine whether a principal would be allowed or denied. Critical for diagnosing AccessDenied errors during incidents.
synthetic_queries: why is this getting access denied
can this role perform that action

tools.name: secrets_manager_get_secret_value
tools.description: Retrieve a secret value from AWS Secrets Manager. Used by applications to fetch database passwords, API keys, third-party credentials at runtime.

tools.name: ssm_get_parameter
tools.description: Retrieve a single parameter from SSM Parameter Store. Used for configuration values, feature flags, environment-specific settings.

tools.name: ssm_start_session
tools.description: Start an SSM Session Manager session to a specific EC2 instance without SSH. Used for incident response and on-instance diagnostics without inbound network exposure.
synthetic_queries: ssh into the instance without ssh
open a shell on the box

tools.name: ec2_describe_instances
tools.description: Describe EC2 instances, optionally filtered by tag or state. Used to enumerate running hosts and check instance health.

tools.name: ec2_describe_volumes
tools.description: Describe EBS volumes including attachment state and IOPS configuration. Used during storage-related performance incidents.

tools.name: elbv2_describe_target_health
tools.description: Get target health status for an ALB or NLB target group — which targets are healthy, unhealthy, or draining. Critical for diagnosing intermittent 5xx errors.
synthetic_queries: which targets are failing health checks

tools.name: kinesis_get_records
tools.description: Read records from a Kinesis data stream shard iterator. Used to inspect real-time event data and diagnose stream-consumer lag.

tools.name: cloudtrail_lookup_events
tools.description: Look up CloudTrail events by attribute (event name, user, resource ID). Used to audit configuration changes and trace who-did-what-when during incidents.

tools.name: ses_send_email
tools.description: Send an email through SES with a specified template or raw body. Used for transactional emails, incident reports, and customer notifications.
